title: Internal Errors: column relationships mismatch
description: ### What happens? After executing the query, the database instance entered an invalid state, preventing subsequent queries. This appears to be a bug introduced in version v1.2.0(in function ExpressionExecutor::Execute), where column relationships cannot be correctly matched, leading to the error. In v1.1.1, there was a different error： But I couldn't reproduce the scenario in v1.2.1. The bugfix list doesn't clearly mention similar issues. Was this addressed by PR https://github.com/duckdb/duckdb/pull/16111 in the v1.2.1 release notes? ### To Reproduce ```sql CREATE TABLE test1(a SMALLINT, b VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE test2(a INTEGER, b VARCHAR); INSERT INTO test1 VALUES(5, 'abdfeed'); INSERT INTO test2 VALUES(12,'word23'),(13,'feed32feed'); SELECT * FROM test1 WHERE (a, b) in( SELECT a, b FROM test2 ); ``` ### OS: Linux x86_64 ### DuckDB Version: v1.2.0 ### DuckDB Client: CLI ### Hardware: _No response_ ### Full Name: Amore ### Affiliation: Software engineer ### What is the latest build you tested with? If possible, we recommend testing with the latest nightly build. I have tested with a source build ### Did you include all relevant data sets for reproducing the issue? Yes ### Did you include all code required to reproduce the issue? - [x] Yes, I have ### Did you include all relevant configuration (e.g., CPU architecture, Python version, Linux distribution) to reproduce the issue? - [x] Yes, I have
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test3(x FLOAT, y BOOLEAN);
CREATE TABLE test4(x DOUBLE PRECISION, y VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL);
INSERT INTO test3 VALUES(1.5, TRUE), (NULL, FALSE);
SELECT * FROM test3 WHERE (x, y) IN (SELECT x, y FROM test4 WHERE y IS NOT NULL);
key_bug_features: Column relationships cannot be correctly matched
